ESCAPE_CHARACTER.description: A single backslash preceding any syntax character.
ESCAPE_CHARACTER.behavior: Prevents the lexer from interpreting the subsequent character as a syntax operator.
ESCAPE_CHARACTER.outcome: Outputs `<bracket>` without initiating an AST node capture.

INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.context: Top-level raw string inputs (prior to AST evaluation).
INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.description: A line starting and ending with one or more hash `#` characters, optionally surrounding a title, with optional whitespace padding.
INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.behavior: Splits multi-line strings into routed blocks. Accessed via positive (Preamble + Block) or negative (Block Only) integer routing.
INDEXED_BLOCK_ROUTER.outcome: Isolates 'Text block here.' for injection into the engine.

LORA_WRAPPER.description: Square brackets enclosing a literal group name, a colon, and the native ComfyUI `<lora:...>` string.
LORA_WRAPPER.behavior: Protects native ComfyUI syntax from macro evaluation, routes the payload to the Trace State Object under `group_name`, and strips the bracket wrapper for final output.

2_STATE_MUTATIONS_DEFINITIONS.ORTHOGONAL_MATRIX_RULES: Definitions follow [CLASS][PATTERN_TYPE][STRENGTH] syntax. They must appear at the start of a parsed line or segment.

CLASS_PRE_PATTERN.description: A colon followed immediately by a left angle bracket.
CLASS_PRE_PATTERN.behavior: Unbounded Regex/Literal evaluated Left-to-Right against raw strings before AST lexing.

CLASS_BOUNDED.behavior: Evaluated exclusively within `< >` AST macro boundaries.

CLASS_POST_PATTERN.description: A colon followed immediately by a right angle bracket.
CLASS_POST_PATTERN.behavior: Unbounded Regex/Literal evaluated Left-to-Right against flattened strings after AST resolution.

STRENGTH_STRONG.description: A single colon between the pattern and the value.
STRENGTH_STRONG.behavior: Pushed to the Tail of the Context Stack (Evaluated First / Overrides).

STRENGTH_WEAK.description: Two colons between the pattern and the value.
STRENGTH_WEAK.behavior: Pushed to the Head of the Context Stack (Evaluated Last / Default).

PATTERN_TYPE_REGEX.description: A string enclosed entirely by forward slashes.
PATTERN_TYPE_REGEX.behavior: Parses the enclosed string as a Regular Expression. Valid for both the matching key/pattern and the replacement value.

PATTERN_TYPE_LITERAL.description: Any string not entirely enclosed by forward slashes.
PATTERN_TYPE_LITERAL.behavior: Parses as an exact string match (for keys) or literal replacement (for values).

EXAMPLES.description: Bounded, Strong, Literal Key, Literal Value.
Pre-pattern, Weak, Regex Key, Regex Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Fallback pre-pattern replacing 'dark' with ' light'. Native regex escape sequences and group backreferences are fully supported during the substitution pass.

EXAMPLES.description: Bounded, Strong, Literal Key, Regex Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: Overrides `<man>` to output `gentleman` followed by a literal newline. The native python regex engine handles the `\n` escape parsing automatically.

EXAMPLES.description: Post-pattern, Strong, Regex Key, Literal Value.
EXAMPLES.outcome: A cleanup post-pattern that finds multiple adjacent spaces and replaces them with a single space.

STANDARD_INVOCATION.description: A literal token key surrounded by angle brackets.
STANDARD_INVOCATION.behavior: Searches Context Stack Right-to-Left. Replaces token with resolved AST of matching definition.

PARAMETERIZED_INVOCATION.description: A key, followed by a pipe `|`, followed by argument definitions separated by pipes, enclosed in angle brackets.
PARAMETERIZED_INVOCATION.behavior: Pushes `arg1` as a STRONG definition to local scope, evaluates `<key>`, then pops `arg1`.

DEFAULT_PARAMETER_INVOCATION.description: Same as parameterized, but using a double colon `::` for the argument definition.
DEFAULT_PARAMETER_INVOCATION.behavior: Pushes `arg1` as a WEAK definition to local scope.
DEFAULT_PARAMETER_INVOCATION.outcome: Evaluates `subject` with `color` as `yellow` ONLY IF no higher-priority definition exists.

ATOMIC_ASSIGN_AND_RETURN.description: A literal key and literal value separated by a single colon, enclosed in angle brackets.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_AND_RETURN.behavior: Simultaneously pushes `key:value` as a STRONG definition (Tail of stack) and resolves the AST node to `value`.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_AND_RETURN.outcome: Outputs 'The gentleman arrived' and registers `man:gentleman` as a strong definition for subsequent sibling nodes.

ATOMIC_ASSIGN_DEFAULT_AND_RETURN.description: A literal key and literal value separated by a double colon, enclosed in angle brackets.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_DEFAULT_AND_RETURN.behavior: Simultaneously pushes `key:value` as a WEAK definition (Head of stack) and resolves the AST node to `value`.
ATOMIC_ASSIGN_DEFAULT_AND_RETURN.outcome: Outputs 'The guy arrived' and registers `man:guy` as a weak definition for subsequent sibling nodes.

TRANSPARENT_BLOCK_DUMMY_ROOT.description: An opening angle bracket followed immediately by a pipe `|`, followed by macro invocations separated by pipes, closed by an angle bracket.
TRANSPARENT_BLOCK_DUMMY_ROOT.behavior: The isolated `|` suppresses standard `ENTER_SCOPE`/`EXIT_SCOPE` markers. Macros share a continuous local scope block.
TRANSPARENT_BLOCK_DUMMY_ROOT.outcome: Definitions hoisted by `<SetColors>` remain active and accessible during `<DrawScene>` evaluation, popping only when the outer `< >` terminates.

STANDARD_RANDOM.description: Multiple string options separated by pipes `|`, enclosed in curly braces.
STANDARD_RANDOM.behavior: Selects one option using the deterministic path-hashed PRNG.

QUANTITY_MODIFIER.description: An integer followed by a double dollar sign `$$`, preceding the piped options inside curly braces.
QUANTITY_MODIFIER.behavior: Selects `qty` number of options without replacement. Default separator is ', '.

CUSTOM_SEPARATOR.description: An integer, `$$`, a literal string separator, `$$`, and the piped options.

DISCRETE_SEQUENCE_GENERATOR.description: Colon-separated integers preceding the `$$` in a multi-value group.
DISCRETE_SEQUENCE_GENERATOR.behavior: Generates a discrete list of valid indices/quantities to be fed into the PRNG prior to item selection.
DISCRETE_SEQUENCE_GENERATOR.outcome: Randomly selects 1, 2, or 3 items from the pool.

POSITIONAL_ARGUMENTS.description: Numeric digits enclosed in angle brackets.
POSITIONAL_ARGUMENTS.behavior: Accessing parameterized arguments by integer index rather than explicit key string.

REGEX_CAPTURE_INTEROPERABILITY.description: Native regex capture groups and backreferences used within macro boundaries.
REGEX_CAPTURE_INTEROPERABILITY.behavior: Seamless interoperability between macro argument scopes and native regex capture groups. For example, mapping named regex capture groups `(?P<name>...)` on a key pattern directly to local `<name>` invocations within the replacement value.

INDEX_MASKING.description: A leading integer (or range) prefixed with a dollar sign `$`, followed by standard quantity modifiers.
INDEX_MASKING.behavior: Restricting the PRNG pool to specific indices of the provided options before selection.

CONTEXT_APPEND_OPERATOR.description: A colon followed by a pipe.
CONTEXT_APPEND_OPERATOR.behavior: Concatenating a new value to an existing key array rather than standard priority shadowing.
